author: Frank Vanderham
message:
Raise minimum Qt requirement to 6.0 (#1155)

- remove Qt5/Qt6 compatibility shims and legacy Qt5 references
- switch build/lint/test workflows to Qt6 across Linux, macOS, and Windows
- update CMake/configuration and apply related Qt6 API/deprecation cleanups

author: Heiko Becker
message:
Fix building the tests with clang

Clang (I only tested with versions >= 18) complains about narrowing
from long long int (qsizetype) to int on my platform. gcc accepts it,
but issues a warning. So just use qsizetype instead of int as type of
the variable as well.

author: Heiko Becker
message:
Drop obsolete if based on an old cmake version

CMake >= 3.11 is required since the previous commit.

author: Heiko Becker
message:
Update msgpack(-c) to 6.1.0 and simplify fetching it

msgpack split its mixed C/C++ tarball into two separate ones starting
with 4.0 [1][2]. Since 6.0.0 the C part uses msgpack-c as a name for
everything. This is where the requirement for 6.0.0 comes from, but it
has been released over two years ago and I'd expect it to be available
for everybody who wants to use -DUSE_SYSTEM_MSGPACK=TRUE.
It allows to just use the imported target from msgpack-c's cmake config
file and to simplify the cmake code a bit.
This fixes the build with CMake >= 4.0, which has removed compatibility
with CMake < 3.5 while msgpack 3.2.0 sets
"CMAKE_MINIMUM_REQUIRED (VERSION 2.8.12)".
Also raise the cmake requirement to 3.11 for FetchContent.

[1] https://github.com/msgpack/msgpack-c/pull/876
[2] https://github.com/msgpack/msgpack-c/pull/878
[3] https://github.com/msgpack/msgpack-c/pull/1053

author: Wolfgang Schafzahl
message:
Fix CloseEvent, Forceful Exit

When the user or the Window Manager tries to close the application
a second time after nvim displays the "Yes Save/No Save/Cancel" prompt,
the application will now exit gracefully and not save the unsaved
changes.

author: Wolfgang Schafzahl
message: Fix CloseEvent and rpcnotify()

author: Wolfgang Schafzahl
message:
Fix CloseEvent

Fix unnecessary calls that trigger CloseEvents of the Main Window.
If there is unsaved data, the CloseEvent will now stall until the
user decides to Save/NotSave/Cancel. This stall will tell the
Session Manager that the Nvim process is blocking the close request.

author: John Gehrig
message:
Update GitHub Actions upload-artifact to v4 (#1130)

The version of upload-artifact we're currently using is deprecated:
https://github.blog/changelog/2024-02-13-deprecation-notice-v1-and-v2-of-the-artifact-actions/

We should bump to the latest version.

This also fixes a small typo in the script, where a multiline run '|'
was missing.

author: pastalian
message:
Fix font weight on Qt6 (#1124)

Qt6 has different weight mapping from Qt5. It scales from 1 to 1000.
https://doc.qt.io/qt-6/qfont.html#Weight-enum

author: equalsraf
message:
Try to make builds reproducible

Reproducible builds for neovim-qt are not working because mapping flags
passed to the cmake build are included in the code (through version.h.in).
Meaning that builds with slightly different mapping flags end up being
different; even if no other compiler flags are different.

ref https://github.com/equalsraf/neovim-qt/issues/1111

Adds build option for reproducible builds. Currently only affects the use
of compilation flags in the --version output.

author: Brian Golden
message:
Installation instructions using winget (#1115)

- Update the README with installation instructions using winget.
- Neovim-qt was removed from the Neovim package starting with version `0.10.0` of neovim and must be installed separately now.

Resolves #1108
Resolves #1109
